prose:
Frontier-orbital energies for ethylene (C2H4, 6 atoms) computed at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (chemkit tier 'standard'), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell RKS). Single-point calculation on the user-supplied ethylene.xyz geometry (not re-relaxed at this level). Exact four-center integrals used (no density fitting). Backend: PySCF. SCF converged in 6 cycles. No warnings reported. Integrity check: status=ok, trustworthy=true.

HOMO = −7.6155818 eV (index 7)
LUMO = −0.17986527 eV (index 8)
HOMO–LUMO gap (headline value) = 7.4357165 eV

These are Koopmans-theorem orbital gaps, not excitation or optical gaps.

Additional frontier orbitals:
  HOMO−2 (idx 5) = −11.487503 eV
  HOMO−1 (idx 6) = −9.883854 eV
  LUMO+1 (idx 9) = +1.720103 eV
  LUMO+2 (idx 10) = +2.3802006 eV

Total electronic energy: −78.623486 Hartree.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier [ethylene.xyz] --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output: ethylene_frontier_dft.json
